bugs_found.description: The storage helper accepted raw event/type/certificate segments and traversal-style object keys, so direct callers could build or sign non-canonical R2 paths like '../other.pdf' that violated the expected certificates/{eventId}/{type}/{certificateId}.pdf layout and downstream event/type scoping assumptions.
bugs_found.fix: Validated certificate event/type/id segments before building keys and rejected storage keys with traversal segments, empty path components, control characters, leading or trailing whitespace, backslashes, or overlong lengths before any R2 upload/sign/delete call.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/certificates/storage.test.ts > buildCertificateStorageKey > rejects malformed path segments that would poison certificate storage layout; src/lib/certificates/storage.test.ts > createR2Provider > rejects malformed storage keys before signing URLs

bugs_found.description: getSignedUrl forwarded zero, fractional, and overlong expiry values directly to the presigner, so a caller could mint certificate or export download URLs that lived longer than the app's temporary-access policy instead of failing closed.
bugs_found.fix: Bounded signed URL expiry to safe integers between 1 and 3600 seconds before calling the S3 presigner.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/certificates/storage.test.ts > createR2Provider > rejects non-integer or overlong signed URL expiries

bugs_found.description: withEventScope only rejected falsy event IDs. Any other string, including whitespace-padded, malformed, or oversized UUID-shaped garbage, was accepted and forwarded into UUID-backed query conditions, so any caller that forgot route/body validation could turn this shared scope guard into a DB-layer failure path instead of failing closed at the tenant boundary.
bugs_found.fix: Validated eventId with the canonical event UUID schema before building any SQL condition and added regression coverage to prove malformed event IDs are rejected before eq()/and() are invoked.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/db/with-event-scope.test.ts > rejects invalid eventId; src/lib/db/with-event-scope.mutation-kill.test.ts > rejects malformed event IDs before building any SQL

bugs_found.description: The archive helper exports accepted arbitrary eventId strings. Direct callers could build `events/<payload>/archives/...` storage keys and send malformed, whitespace-padded, or oversized tenant IDs into UUID-backed event lookups before any boundary validation ran.
bugs_found.fix: Added canonical event UUID validation at the archive-module boundary and reused the validated eventId for every exported helper plus archive key generation.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/exports/archive.test.ts > ADVERSARIAL: malformed eventId boundary

bugs_found.description: generateAgendaExcel joined sessions to halls by hallId only. A bad or legacy session row in the active event could therefore leak another event's hall name into the exported agenda if it referenced a foreign hall UUID.
bugs_found.fix: Scoped the joined hall row with withEventScope(halls.eventId, eventId, eq(sessions.hallId, halls.id)) so agenda exports only resolve hall metadata from the active event.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/exports/archive.test.ts > event-scopes joined hall metadata to prevent cross-tenant leakage

bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/exports/emergency-kit.test.ts > generateProgramJson event-scopes joined halls to prevent cross-event leakage

bugs_found.description: EXPORT_TYPES was a normal object, so consumers validating with `type in EXPORT_TYPES` accepted prototype-chain keys like `__proto__`, `constructor`, and `toString` as if they were real export types. Malformed route params could therefore bypass the intended export-type allowlist and fail later instead of being rejected at the boundary.
bugs_found.fix: Rebuilt the registry on a null-prototype object so inherited keys are never treated as valid export types.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/exports/types.test.ts > rejects prototype-chain keys as valid export types

bugs_found.description: The exported registry and its nested metadata objects were mutable process-wide. Any importer could rewrite labels or descriptions and poison later filenames, UI copy, or validation behavior for subsequent requests.
bugs_found.fix: Deep-froze the export registry and every metadata entry so shared runtime state cannot be tampered with after module load.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/exports/types.test.ts > does not allow shared runtime mutation of export metadata

bugs_found.description: The per-event flag helper trusted raw runtime event IDs. Whitespace-padded or otherwise non-canonical UUID strings produced different Redis keys, so reads missed the real flag and fell back to the default `registration_open=true`, while writes could silently create alias keys instead of updating the active event's flag.
bugs_found.fix: Validated canonical event UUIDs inside getEventFlag, getAllEventFlags, and setEventFlag before building Redis keys.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/flags.test.ts > rejects non-canonical event IDs before reading event flags

bugs_found.description: FLAG_DEFAULTS and the exported flag registries were plain mutable objects/arrays. A runtime caller could resolve prototype keys like `__proto__` through the defaults map and receive a truthy object instead of a boolean, and any importer could mutate the allowed flags or defaults for later requests.
bugs_found.fix: Moved defaults and lookup registries onto frozen null-prototype objects, froze the exported flag arrays, and added runtime flag validation before Redis access.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/flags.test.ts > uses immutable null-prototype registries for exported flag metadata

bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/import/csv-import.test.ts > rejects spreadsheet formula payloads in text fields

bugs_found.description: parseCsvString would parse arbitrarily large CSV previews and allowed more than 500 rows even though the server action rejects imports above that limit, letting hostile or oversized files consume client work before failing late.

bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/import/csv-import.test.ts > rejects CSV files that exceed the batch row limit

bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/inngest/bulk-functions.test.ts > rejects forged lock keys that do not match the active event and certificate type

A caller could open the 'resend' circuit, then bypass it with 'resend ' or another unknown variant, which created a parallel namespace that read as closed; oversized names also sprayed unbounded Redis keys.

bugs_found.description: findLogByProviderMessageId queried `notification_log` by `providerMessageId` alone even though the schema only guarantees uniqueness on `(provider, provider_message_id)`. If `evolution_api` and `waba` emitted the same upstream message ID, a WhatsApp webhook could correlate to the other provider's log, append a delivery event, and advance the wrong notification status because the channel check still passed.
bugs_found.fix: Scoped provider-message lookup by both `providerMessageId` and `provider`, and updated webhook ingest to pass the verified provider through that lookup path.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/notifications/delivery-event-queries.mutation-kill.test.ts > scopes the lookup by provider as well as provider message id; src/lib/notifications/webhook-ingest.test.ts > should ignore a WhatsApp webhook that resolves to a different WhatsApp provider log; src/lib/notifications/webhook-ingest.mutation-kill.test.ts > does not correlate a whatsapp webhook across providers that share a message id

Direct callers could inject CRLF-bearing display names or reserved headers like `bcc`, altering outbound provider payloads or forcing provider-level header parse failures.

A malformed present value like `user:segment` or `segment:event-1` could inject extra delimiters and make two distinct notification scopes produce the exact same Redis idempotency key, causing the later send to be suppressed as a duplicate.
bugs_found.fix: Preserved the legacy raw format for ordinary safe identifiers, but URL-encode unsafe key segments before joining them so delimiter-bearing values cannot shift key boundaries or collide with another notification.

A direct caller could reuse another event's key and either mutate that foreign row in place during `upsertLogEntry` or block a new begin-attempt path for the wrong tenant.
bugs_found.fix: Scoped idempotency conflict updates to the matching `eventId` and fail closed when a reserved key belongs to a different event instead of updating or reusing the foreign row.

bugs_found.description: The helper trusted raw tenant and row identifiers plus whitespace-padded idempotency keys. Malformed values reached UUID-backed queries directly, and whitespace variants created parallel idempotency namespaces that bypassed expected duplicate suppression semantics.
bugs_found.fix: Validated canonical event/log IDs at the helper boundary and rejected blank or whitespace-padded idempotency keys before issuing any insert, select, or update.

Two concurrent seed runs against `eventId = null` rows could both observe an empty result and create duplicate active system templates because the existing partial unique index does not prevent duplicate NULL-scoped keys.
bugs_found.fix: Wrapped the seed loop in one transaction and acquired a transaction-scoped Postgres advisory lock before any existence checks so concurrent runs serialize safely.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/notifications/seed-system-templates.adversarial.test.ts > serializes concurrent seed runs so duplicate global templates are not created

bugs_found.description: A mid-loop insert failure left earlier system templates committed because the seeder autocommitted each insert independently, producing a half-seeded global template set after a transient database error or dropped connection.

bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/notifications/seed-system-templates.adversarial.test.ts > rolls back earlier inserts when a later seed fails

bugs_found.description: getTemplateById exposed an optional unscoped lookup path. Any direct caller that knew or guessed a template UUID could omit eventId and read another event's template by primary key, bypassing the module's tenant-isolation contract. bugs_found.fix: Required an explicit event scope (`eventId` or `null` for globals), validated template/event UUIDs up front, and routed event-scoped reads through `withEventScope` so malformed tenant IDs never reach Drizzle query execution.

bugs_found.fix: Replaced the read-then-write increment with an atomic SQL `version_no = version_no + 1` update so concurrent edits cannot race through the same next version.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/notifications/template-queries.adversarial.test.ts > increments template versions atomically on content updates

bugs_found.description: withTimeout forwarded unchecked timeoutMs values into Node's timer API. Overflowed, non-finite, fractional, zero, or negative values are coerced to a near-immediate 1ms timer, so malformed caller input could force provider timeouts and emit overflow warnings instead of honoring the configured delay.
bugs_found.fix: Validated timeoutMs as a safe integer between 1 and Node's maximum supported 32-bit timer delay before creating the AbortController or scheduling the timeout.

bugs_found.description: createTrigger and updateTrigger trusted any UUID-shaped templateId and never verified template ownership. A direct caller could persist an automation trigger in one event that referenced another event's notification template, leaving a poisoned cross-tenant link in production state.
bugs_found.fix: Validated templateId shape and required the referenced template to belong to the active event or be a global default before insert/update.

bugs_found.description: verifyResendSignature never checked svix-timestamp freshness or numeric shape. A captured legitimate Resend webhook could therefore be replayed indefinitely, and even malformed signed timestamp strings were accepted, allowing old deliveries to be reinserted and status handling to rerun long after the original event.
bugs_found.fix: Added fail-closed numeric timestamp validation and a 5-minute freshness window before HMAC comparison so stale or malformed signed Resend requests are rejected at the webhook boundary.

bugs_found.description: pushToDlq trusted runtime input and popFromDlq trusted raw Redis values. A direct caller could enqueue invalid provider/channel/timestamp data, and one malformed queued value later caused popFromDlq to throw, discard already-popped good entries from the return value, and silently lose recoverable webhook deliveries.
bugs_found.fix: Added runtime DLQ entry validation on push/pop, refused malformed queue writes, and changed popFromDlq to skip poison entries while preserving already-popped valid entries.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/notifications/webhook-dlq.adversarial.test.ts > rejects malformed providers before poisoning the DLQ; src/lib/notifications/webhook-dlq.adversarial.test.ts > skips malformed popped entries instead of discarding already-popped valid ones

bugs_found.description: popFromDlq accepted any caller-supplied count, so a direct caller could force arbitrarily large Redis drain batches and unbounded rpop round-trips in one invocation.
bugs_found.fix: Normalized dequeue counts to fail closed on invalid values and capped each batch to 100 entries.

bugs_found.description: webhook-ingest inserted `notification_delivery_events` before the notification_log compare-and-set update, so duplicate, regressive, or replayed webhooks still appended forensic rows even when the status transition was rejected. The same ingest path also trusted malformed provider timestamps, which could turn an otherwise valid signed payload into repeated error/DLQ churn.
bugs_found.fix: Normalized malformed provider timestamps to the current time, moved the status CAS ahead of the forensic insert, and only record a delivery event when the CAS accepts that transition.

bugs_found.description: The webhook parsers trusted inherited properties on payload objects and used a plain-object Resend event registry. A malformed payload with `type: "__proto__"` parsed with `eventType = Object.prototype`, and prototype-backed payload objects parsed as legitimate webhook updates even though the provider data was not actually present as own fields.
bugs_found.fix: Restricted parser inputs to plain/null-prototype records, read only own properties for every nested field, and moved status/event registries to immutable null-prototype maps so prototype-chain keys cannot resolve as valid webhook metadata.

bugs_found.description: captureError only called Sentry.setUser on truthy userIds. In a warm Node process, a later anonymous or system-originated error reused the previous request's Sentry user context, misattributing incidents and security signals to the wrong user.
bugs_found.fix: Always reset the request-scoped Sentry user context by calling setUser({ id }) for authenticated captures and setUser(null) for anonymous/system captures before captureException.

bugs_found.description: cn() delegated object handling to clsx, which iterates inherited enumerable keys. A prototype-backed class dictionary could therefore inject ancestor class names like hidden even when the caller only intended its own keys.

bugs_found.description: cn() used clsx's recursive array traversal. Cyclic inputs or very deep nested arrays could throw RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded and crash render paths that touched the helper.
bugs_found.fix: Flatten class values iteratively, skip cyclic arrays, and cap traversal depth/node count so malformed inputs fail closed instead of overflowing the stack.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/utils.test.ts > does not recurse forever on cyclic class arrays; src/lib/utils.test.ts > fails closed on deeply nested class arrays instead of overflowing the stack
